baseline.stem: A Socratic tutor gives different hints to the same student question on repeated runs. Which change best improves consistency without eliminating all useful variation?
baseline.explanation: Temperature controls sampling variability; lowering it makes pedagogical hints more repeatable.

baseline.stem: A student says, “Just give me the final answer.” Where should the durable rule requiring hints instead of answer leakage be placed?

choices.text: In the system instruction and enforced by policy tests

baseline.explanation: System-level policy plus golden tests is the reliable way to enforce tutor behavior.

baseline.stem: Usage logs show failures only when long conversations exceed the model limit. Which mechanism most directly explains the lost early instructions?

choices.text: The context window cannot contain the full conversation

baseline.explanation: A finite context window can force older content out of the model input.

baseline.stem: The tutor confidently agrees with a student's incorrect algebra claim despite evidence in the prompt. Which failure mode is most specific?
baseline.explanation: Sycophancy is undue agreement with the user, including affirming incorrect claims.

baseline.stem: You are validating a CLI tutor policy before release. Which test set provides the strongest evidence that it resists answer leakage?

choices.text: One friendly prompt at temperature zero
Golden tests covering direct and adversarial requests for final answers
A snapshot of the welcome message

baseline.explanation: Behavioral golden tests should cover normal and adversarial attempts to bypass the policy.

baseline.stem: Retrieved passages are relevant, but the model cites source IDs that were never retrieved. What is the best pipeline correction?

choices.text: Validate output citations against retrieved source IDs

baseline.explanation: A citation post-processor should reject or repair references not present in retrieved context.

baseline.stem: An A/B evaluation asks whether retrieval reduces unsupported claims. Which comparison is valid?

choices.text: The same questions with and without retrieved context, scored for faithfulness

baseline.explanation: Holding questions and generation conditions stable isolates grounding as the treatment.

baseline.stem: A RAG request takes 4.2 seconds. Logs show retrieval takes 0.3 seconds and generation takes 3.7 seconds. Where should optimization begin?
baseline.explanation: Stage-level timing identifies generation as the dominant latency contributor.

baseline.stem: Which ordering correctly represents an end-to-end curriculum RAG request?
baseline.explanation: RAG first retrieves evidence, adds it to model context, then generates.

baseline.stem: The tutor must always support claims with curriculum evidence. Which design provides defense in depth?

choices.text: Citation instructions plus post-generation citation validation
A larger model with no validator
More retrieved chunks regardless of relevance

baseline.explanation: Prompt rules guide generation while deterministic validation enforces source integrity.

reassessment.stem: A hint generator at temperature 0.9 produces creative but pedagogically inconsistent scaffolds. What is the most targeted adjustment?

choices.text: Reduce temperature and rerun a fixed prompt suite
Convert every user message to assistant role

reassessment.explanation: Lower sampling temperature and a fixed evaluation suite directly address consistency.

reassessment.stem: A tutor follows “reveal answers” text embedded in a student's message even though the product requires Socratic guidance. Which remediation is strongest?

choices.text: Put the non-leakage policy in system context and add adversarial regression cases

reassessment.explanation: High-priority instructions and adversarial tests protect the intended policy.

reassessment.stem: A 30-page lesson and full chat history do not fit in one request. Which response reflects correct LLM constraints?

choices.text: The model reads files outside its context automatically
Select or summarize relevant content so tokens fit the context window
The assistant role stores unlimited memory

reassessment.explanation: Only input within the context window is available, so selection or compression is required.

reassessment.stem: During misconception diagnosis, the tutor invents a theorem that is absent from all supplied material. How should this be classified?
reassessment.explanation: Fabricating unsupported information is hallucination.

reassessment.stem: Which release check best verifies that policy changes did not turn a Socratic tutor into an answer bot?

choices.text: Run golden conversations asserting hints and refusals across attack variants

reassessment.explanation: Golden conversation assertions test the actual pedagogical behavior.

reassessment.stem: A generated response cites [unit-12], but retrieval returned only [unit-4] and [unit-9]. What should the post-processor do?

choices.text: Accept it because the format is valid
Reject or regenerate because the cited ID is not in retrieved evidence

reassessment.explanation: Citation validity requires membership in the retrieved source set, not merely correct syntax.

reassessment.stem: A team claims grounding improved after testing RAG on easy questions and the baseline on hard questions. What flaw invalidates the claim?

choices.text: The treatment did not use matched questions
Twenty questions is always too many

reassessment.explanation: A matched A/B design is needed to attribute differences to retrieval.

reassessment.stem: Profiling shows vector lookup is 2.6 seconds and generation is 0.8 seconds. Which action follows the evidence?
reassessment.explanation: The measured retrieval stage is the dominant bottleneck.

reassessment.stem: A developer passes the student's question directly to the LLM, then searches for sources afterward. Why is this not RAG?

choices.text: Sources must be retrieved before generation so they can augment context

reassessment.explanation: Post-hoc search cannot ground generation that already occurred.

reassessment.stem: Citation compliance is high in prompt-only tests but drops under adversarial inputs. Which architecture is most robust?

choices.text: Enforce source IDs in the prompt and verify them deterministically afterward

reassessment.explanation: Deterministic output validation complements prompt-level instructions.

baseline.stem: A tutor observes an incorrect step, selects a misconception-specific hint, presents it, then checks the next attempt. Which loop is being implemented?
baseline.explanation: The ITS loop senses learner state, decides an intervention, acts, and assesses its effect.

baseline.stem: Two learners receive the same worksheet despite different mastery evidence. Which mastery-learning change is most appropriate?

choices.text: Advance both on a fixed calendar
Adapt practice and progression to demonstrated mastery

baseline.explanation: Mastery learning varies support and time based on demonstrated understanding.

baseline.stem: Which feature most clearly distinguishes a structured cognitive tutor from a generic chat interface?

choices.text: A student model tied to domain skills and targeted feedback

baseline.explanation: Classical ITS architecture explicitly models domain knowledge and learner state.

baseline.stem: An adaptive course gives a short check after each concept and routes learners to remediation when needed. What pattern does this illustrate?
baseline.explanation: Frequent evidence is used to select the learner's next activity.

baseline.stem: A team automates hint selection but leaves final mastery judgments to an instructor. How should the tutor-loop diagram represent this?

choices.text: Label decision components with their human or automated owner
Replace the loop with a database table

baseline.explanation: An architecture diagram should make automation boundaries and human decisions explicit.

baseline.stem: A standard says “understand fractions.” Which representation is most useful in a machine-readable skill graph?

choices.text: One broad node named understand fractions
Atomic skills such as compare fractions and find common denominators

baseline.explanation: Atomic, assessable skills support prerequisites, diagnosis, and sequencing.

baseline.stem: Adding A→B, B→C, and C→A to a prerequisite graph causes what critical problem?

choices.text: It creates a cycle, so no valid topological order exists

baseline.explanation: Prerequisite dependencies must be acyclic for sequencing by topological order.

baseline.stem: Students who add denominators when adding fractions need targeted remediation. How should this knowledge be modeled?

choices.text: As a misconception linked to diagnostic evidence and a remediation skill

baseline.explanation: Explicit misconception nodes connect observed errors to corrective instruction.

baseline.stem: A graph file sometimes stores difficulty as text and sometimes as a number. What should catch this before runtime?
baseline.explanation: A schema enforces consistent types and required fields in graph artifacts.

baseline.stem: A learner has mastered A. B requires A, while C requires unmastered D. Which next-skill policy is sound?

choices.text: Recommend B because its prerequisites are satisfied
Recommend C because it is later alphabetically
Recommend D only if it creates a cycle

baseline.explanation: A sequencer should select unmastered skills whose prerequisite set is mastered.

reassessment.stem: After a learner answers, the system updates a mastery estimate but never changes its next hint. Which tutor-loop link is missing?

choices.text: Assessment evidence informing the next decision

reassessment.explanation: Assessment must feed the decision stage for the loop to adapt.

reassessment.stem: One learner needs three extra examples before meeting criterion; another advances immediately. Which principle justifies the difference?

choices.text: Mastery learning permits variable support and time
All learners must receive identical pacing
Assessment should occur only at course end

reassessment.explanation: Mastery learning adapts path and support while holding learning goals stable.

reassessment.stem: A chatbot answers domain questions but tracks neither skills nor learner progress. Which ITS capability is absent?
reassessment.explanation: ITS behavior depends on structured knowledge and learner-state models.

reassessment.stem: A course branches only after a scored concept check. Why is this stronger than branching by page-view count?

choices.text: The branch uses evidence of learning rather than mere activity
Page views are always invalid data
Branching eliminates the need for objectives

reassessment.explanation: Formative performance is more directly connected to mastery than exposure.

reassessment.stem: An instructor approves advancement while software selects practice items. What is the clearest architecture documentation?

choices.text: Mark item selection automated and advancement human-controlled

reassessment.explanation: Ownership labels make mixed human-machine tutor loops auditable.

reassessment.stem: “Can solve quadratic equations” is too coarse to diagnose where learners fail. Which redesign helps most?

choices.text: Split it into assessable component skills with stable IDs
Rename it with a longer sentence

reassessment.explanation: Atomic skills expose precise prerequisite and misconception relationships.

reassessment.stem: A new edge makes every candidate next skill depend indirectly on itself. Which validation should fail?

choices.text: Cycle detection for the prerequisite DAG

reassessment.explanation: A cycle violates the DAG invariant and prevents valid sequencing.

reassessment.stem: A learner repeatedly treats area as perimeter. Which graph addition best supports future diagnosis?

choices.text: A misconception node linked to the error pattern and area remediation
An isolated note with no skill link

reassessment.explanation: Linked misconception representations make observed errors actionable.

reassessment.stem: A curriculum import omits required skill IDs but remains syntactically valid JSON. Which control should reject it?

choices.text: A JSON Schema requiring id fields

reassessment.explanation: Syntax validity alone does not enforce required domain fields; schema validation does.

reassessment.stem: Skills X and Y are unmastered. X has all prerequisites mastered; Y depends on Z, which is not mastered. What should a prerequisite-aware recommender choose?
reassessment.explanation: X is currently reachable in a valid topological learning path.

baseline.stem: Credentials for a new AuraDB instance are needed locally. Which repository practice is safe?

choices.text: Commit variable names in .env.example and keep secrets untracked

baseline.explanation: Example environment files document configuration without exposing actual secrets.

baseline.stem: You need all Skill nodes with difficulty at least 3 and their prerequisites. Which Cypher features are central?

choices.text: MATCH relationship patterns plus WHERE filtering

baseline.explanation: MATCH describes nodes and edges; WHERE constrains difficulty.

baseline.stem: A seed script runs twice and duplicates every Skill node. Which write strategy best prevents this?

choices.text: Use MERGE keyed by stable skill ID

baseline.explanation: MERGE on a unique domain key makes repeated seeding idempotent.

baseline.stem: A Python service needs to count Skill nodes reliably. Which driver pattern is appropriate?

choices.text: Open a managed session, run a parameterized query, and close resources
Build queries by concatenating user input
Store the count in the password

baseline.explanation: The official driver with managed sessions and parameters is the safe integration path.

baseline.stem: skills.json and prerequisites.jsonl must populate Neo4j in a repeatable setup. What is the best deliverable?

choices.text: A documented seed script mapping IDs to nodes and edges
A screenshot of an empty graph
An LLM prompt that guesses edges

baseline.explanation: A scripted import makes graph setup reproducible and auditable.

baseline.stem: In BKT, a learner occasionally answers correctly despite not knowing the skill. Which parameter models this?
baseline.explanation: Guess is the probability of a correct response while unmastered.

baseline.stem: A mastered learner makes a careless incorrect response. Which BKT parameter prevents the model from treating this as impossible?
baseline.explanation: Slip captures incorrect responses from a learner in the known state.

baseline.stem: A policy advances learners when posterior mastery exceeds 0.95. What should happen when an update crosses that threshold?

choices.text: Sequence to a successor or reduce scaffolding according to policy

baseline.explanation: A mastery threshold should trigger an explicit instructional decision.

baseline.stem: A question requires both fraction addition and common denominators. Which representation captures this multi-skill requirement?

choices.text: A q-matrix row linked to both skills

baseline.explanation: A q-matrix maps each item to one or more latent skills.

baseline.stem: A correct response is observed. Which BKT update order is conceptually sound?

choices.text: Condition prior mastery on the observation, then apply learning transition
Set mastery to one regardless of guess

baseline.explanation: BKT uses evidence likelihoods for the posterior and then models learning between opportunities.

reassessment.stem: A teammate proposes checking an Aura password into setup documentation so CI can connect. What should you require instead?

choices.text: Secret injection through environment configuration, with placeholders in examples
Putting it in a public issue

reassessment.explanation: Credentials belong in secret storage, while example files contain placeholders.

reassessment.stem: You need prerequisite names only for advanced skills tagged algebra. What query structure fits?

choices.text: MATCH Skill-to-prerequisite patterns and use WHERE for difficulty and tag
CREATE new prerequisites for every match
Return all nodes and filter by eye

reassessment.explanation: Pattern matching and predicates express graph traversal plus property filters.

reassessment.stem: An import job may be retried after a timeout. How should skill nodes be written to avoid duplicates?

choices.text: MERGE using the curriculum skill ID
Generate a new label each retry

reassessment.explanation: Stable-key MERGE supports safe retries.

reassessment.stem: A search term comes from a student. Which Neo4j Python implementation minimizes injection risk?

choices.text: Pass the term as a query parameter in a managed session
Concatenate it into the Cypher string

reassessment.explanation: Parameterized Cypher separates data from query syntax.

reassessment.stem: A new developer must recreate the curriculum graph from source files. Which artifact gives the strongest reproducibility?

choices.text: An idempotent seed script plus documented connection steps
A verbal description of manual edits

reassessment.explanation: Executable, documented setup can be repeated and verified.

reassessment.stem: A novice has a 20% chance of answering correctly by chance. Which BKT value should reflect 0.20?
reassessment.explanation: Guess quantifies correct observations in the unmastered state.

reassessment.stem: Even experts make about 5% careless errors on an item type. Where does 0.05 belong in BKT?
reassessment.explanation: Slip is the error likelihood conditional on mastery.

reassessment.stem: Mastery rises from 0.93 to 0.96 under a 0.95 advancement rule. Which behavior is aligned with the policy?

choices.text: Record the crossing and unlock an eligible next skill
Force five more identical hints regardless of policy

reassessment.explanation: Threshold crossings should cause the documented sequencing or support change.

reassessment.stem: An item tests graph traversal and Cypher filtering together. How should a multi-skill model encode it?

choices.text: Mark both skill columns for that item in the q-matrix
Use latency as the only skill

reassessment.explanation: The q-matrix explicitly links an item to every required latent skill.

reassessment.stem: Why should one correct answer not force BKT mastery to 100%?

choices.text: The answer may be a guess, so Bayesian evidence updates probability rather than certainty

reassessment.explanation: Nonzero guess probability means a correct observation is informative but not conclusive.

baseline.stem: A query contains an exact rare course code that dense retrieval often misses. Which hybrid component is especially useful?
baseline.explanation: Sparse lexical retrieval excels at exact rare terms.

baseline.stem: In a hybrid score alpha*dense + (1-alpha)*sparse, what does testing alpha 0, 0.5, and 1 establish?

choices.text: Sparse-only, blended, and dense-only reference points

baseline.explanation: Endpoint and midpoint tests reveal each retriever's contribution.

baseline.stem: A team picks alpha after reading five successful outputs. What is the stronger tuning method?

choices.text: Benchmark candidate alphas on a labeled retrieval set
Tune on production failures only and report the same set

baseline.explanation: A labeled benchmark supports systematic and repeatable weight selection.

baseline.stem: After replacing vector-only search with hybrid retrieval, which existing control must still pass?
baseline.explanation: Retriever changes must preserve downstream grounding guarantees.

baseline.stem: BM25 and dense scores have very different numeric ranges. What should happen before a simple weighted blend?

choices.text: Calibrate or normalize scores so alpha has interpretable effect

baseline.explanation: Unaligned scales can cause one retriever to dominate regardless of alpha.

baseline.stem: An educator asks for themes spanning the entire curriculum graph. Which GraphRAG mode is the better starting point?

choices.text: Local search around one entity only

baseline.explanation: Global search aggregates community-level summaries for corpus-wide questions.

baseline.stem: A generated community report attributes a concept to the wrong source entity. What is the correct quality action?

choices.text: Compare reports with source entities and correct factual inaccuracies

baseline.explanation: Community summaries need factual review against their source graph.

baseline.stem: Why attach community_id metadata when indexing community summaries?

choices.text: To filter, trace, and link retrieved summaries to graph communities

baseline.explanation: Metadata preserves identity and supports targeted retrieval and provenance.

baseline.stem: What is the main purpose of COMMUNITY_OF edges from skills to detected communities?

choices.text: Connect curriculum skill nodes with GraphRAG thematic structure

baseline.explanation: The edges align graph communities with the existing skill graph.

baseline.stem: A summary omits several key source entities. Which evaluation signal is most relevant?
baseline.explanation: Context recall measures whether required source information is represented.

reassessment.stem: Students search for “BIO-417,” but semantic neighbors outrank the exact course. Which retrieval adjustment addresses this case?

choices.text: Remove the course code from documents

reassessment.explanation: Exact identifiers are a strength of lexical retrieval.

reassessment.stem: At alpha=1 results are semantic; at alpha=0 they are lexical. What does alpha=0.5 test?

choices.text: A balanced blend, assuming score scales are aligned

reassessment.explanation: The midpoint combines both score sources when properly normalized.

reassessment.stem: Alpha 0.7 wins on the same examples used to invent the queries. What additional evidence is needed?

choices.text: Performance on held-out labeled retrieval queries

reassessment.explanation: Held-out evaluation checks whether tuning generalizes.

reassessment.stem: Hybrid retrieval improves recall but generated citations now point outside the returned set. Which regression blocks release?
reassessment.explanation: Grounding controls remain mandatory after retrieval integration.

reassessment.stem: Dense scores cluster near 0.8 while BM25 ranges from 0 to 30. A raw 50/50 sum behaves mostly lexical. Why?

choices.text: The score scales were not normalized or calibrated

reassessment.explanation: Magnitude differences overwhelm nominal weights.

reassessment.stem: A dean asks, “What major instructional themes recur across all departments?” Which retrieval plan fits?

choices.text: Aggregate community reports with global search
Traverse one student's local neighborhood only

reassessment.explanation: Corpus-wide thematic questions are suited to global community aggregation.

reassessment.stem: Three polished community summaries contain invented relationships. What review criterion should take priority?

choices.text: Factual consistency with source entities and edges

reassessment.explanation: Fluency cannot substitute for factual grounding.

reassessment.stem: A retrieved summary cannot be traced back to its originating cluster. Which ingestion omission likely caused this?
reassessment.explanation: Community metadata provides the traceable link.

reassessment.stem: GraphRAG clusters exist separately from Neo4j curriculum skills. Which artifact integrates the two models?

choices.text: Edges linking skills to their communities

reassessment.explanation: Explicit relationships map detected communities onto domain skill nodes.

reassessment.stem: A report covers 4 of 10 required source concepts accurately. Which weakness dominates?
reassessment.explanation: Most relevant source concepts were omitted.

baseline.stem: A local editor launches a tutor tool as a child process with no network listener. Which MCP transport best fits?
baseline.explanation: stdio is well suited to local process-to-process MCP communication.

baseline.stem: In an MCP tutor, which component typically coordinates model interaction and one or more server connections?
baseline.explanation: The host application manages model context and MCP client connections to servers.

baseline.stem: A server offers get_skill_info only when the client declares compatible tool support. What protocol mechanism enables this?
baseline.explanation: Initialization capabilities let peers agree on supported protocol features.

baseline.stem: update_mastery accepts arbitrary fields and writes directly to student records. What is the first design improvement?

choices.text: Define a strict input schema and authorize least-privilege writes

baseline.explanation: Schema validation and least privilege constrain a sensitive mutation tool.

baseline.stem: Curriculum content is read-only and addressable, while recomputing mastery performs an action. How should MCP expose them?

choices.text: Curriculum as resources; mastery operation as a tool

baseline.explanation: Resources represent contextual data; tools perform callable operations.

baseline.stem: A tutor needs skill data, reasons about it, calls a tool, then incorporates the returned record into its next step. Which trace fits ReAct?
baseline.explanation: ReAct interleaves reasoning, external action, and observed results.

baseline.stem: A malformed tool causes the agent to call itself indefinitely. Which guard most directly limits damage?
baseline.explanation: A hard step budget guarantees the loop terminates.

baseline.stem: An MCP call hangs and blocks every tutor session. What control should wrap each invocation?

choices.text: A timeout with graceful failure handling

baseline.explanation: Per-tool timeouts prevent hung dependencies from stalling the agent.

baseline.stem: A failed session log says only “agent error.” Which additional fields are needed to diagnose tool behavior?

choices.text: Tool name, inputs, outputs or error, timestamp, and duration

baseline.explanation: Structured invocation details reconstruct the tool trace and latency.

baseline.stem: The fifth ReAct step times out. What should a robust tutor do?

choices.text: Stop gracefully, record the cause, and return a safe fallback

baseline.explanation: Guardrails should produce bounded, observable, honest failure behavior.

reassessment.stem: A desktop tutor bundles an MCP server and starts it on demand on the same machine. Which transport avoids unnecessary network exposure?
reassessment.explanation: Local stdio communication is simple and does not require an exposed port.

reassessment.stem: One tutor application connects to separate curriculum and analytics MCP servers. Where do the corresponding clients live?

choices.text: Inside the host application coordinating those connections

reassessment.explanation: A host may create multiple MCP clients, generally one per server connection.

reassessment.stem: A client assumes prompts support without checking server initialization data and then fails. What was skipped?
reassessment.explanation: Peers must use negotiated capabilities rather than assuming optional features.

reassessment.stem: A tool intended to update one learner can write every database table. Which security principle is violated?
reassessment.explanation: The tool has broader authority than its task requires.

reassessment.stem: A lesson document should be browsable without side effects, but submit_attempt changes state. Which mapping is correct?

choices.text: Lesson as a resource; submit_attempt as a tool
Lesson as a destructive tool only

reassessment.explanation: Read-only context and executable actions have distinct MCP primitives.

reassessment.stem: The agent decides it lacks prerequisite data, invokes get_skill_info, and receives a skill record. What is the received record in a ReAct trace?
reassessment.explanation: Tool output is the observation used by subsequent reasoning.

reassessment.stem: A tool keeps returning incomplete data and the agent repeatedly retries. Which invariant ensures bounded execution?
reassessment.explanation: A maximum step budget bounds repeated action-observation cycles.

reassessment.stem: An external skill service sometimes takes 40 seconds, but tutor latency budget allows 3 seconds. What should the client enforce?

choices.text: A roughly 3-second tool timeout and fallback path

reassessment.explanation: The timeout should align dependency behavior with the product latency budget.

reassessment.stem: Two tools share a name in logs and one is slow. Which trace data distinguishes the failing invocation?

choices.text: Invocation ID, server/tool identity, input, result, timestamp, and duration

reassessment.explanation: Correlated structured fields make individual calls diagnosable.

reassessment.stem: A timeout occurs after the agent has already used four steps. Which response is trustworthy?

choices.text: Report bounded failure or use a declared safe fallback, preserving the trace

reassessment.explanation: Reliable agents fail transparently within configured limits.

baseline.stem: A sprint must integrate graph, knowledge tracing, GraphRAG, and MCP. Which planning artifact makes completion testable?

choices.text: Acceptance criteria for the unified student flow

baseline.explanation: Acceptance criteria define observable integration outcomes.

baseline.stem: Which component should coordinate graph lookup, mastery update, retrieval, and tool use for one request?
baseline.explanation: The orchestrator composes subsystem calls into the end-to-end flow.

baseline.stem: Unit tests pass for every module, but student sessions fail when modules interact. What test is missing?

choices.text: End-to-end session simulations with realistic fixtures

baseline.explanation: Integration failures require tests across subsystem boundaries.

baseline.stem: GraphRAG is temporarily unavailable. Which response demonstrates graceful degradation?

choices.text: Fall back to vector-only RAG and mark the degraded path

baseline.explanation: A known fallback preserves partial service without hiding the failure.

baseline.stem: Graph, retrieval, and MCP throw unrelated raw exceptions to the UI. What architecture improves consistency?

choices.text: A TutorError hierarchy mapped to stable error responses

baseline.explanation: A domain error hierarchy normalizes failure handling across modules.

baseline.stem: A tutor answer is slow, but a single request duration cannot reveal why. What instrumentation is needed?

choices.text: Child spans for retrieval, LLM, and MCP operations

baseline.explanation: Nested spans expose stage-level timing and errors.

baseline.stem: Which values are best stored as tutor-specific trace metadata for later slicing?
baseline.explanation: Instructional state metadata enables analysis without becoming a secret.

baseline.stem: A RAGAS faithfulness value is computed after a session. How should it be represented in observability?

choices.text: As a score linked to the completed trace

baseline.explanation: Evaluation metrics belong as trace-associated scores.

baseline.stem: Ten sessions include three suspicious tool sequences. What is the best next action?

choices.text: Replay traces, annotate anomalies, and record evidence

baseline.explanation: Session review plus annotation supports root-cause analysis.

baseline.stem: A weekly dashboard shows average latency only. Why should p95 and quality scores also be included?

choices.text: Averages hide tail pain, and speed alone does not show tutor quality

baseline.explanation: Tail latency and quality metrics provide complementary production signals.

baseline.stem: To establish a cost baseline, what should be measured?

choices.text: Token and API cost per representative multi-turn session

baseline.explanation: Per-session usage makes costs attributable and comparable.

baseline.stem: The same curriculum chunks are embedded on every request. Which optimization cuts cost without changing retrieval semantics?

choices.text: Cache embeddings by stable content identity

baseline.explanation: Deterministic embedding reuse avoids redundant API calls.

baseline.stem: Intent routing is simple, but nuanced hints need stronger reasoning. Which model policy is cost-aware?

choices.text: Use a smaller model for routing and reserve the larger model for hints
Use the largest model for every step
Route with no model and no rules

baseline.explanation: Task-based model routing concentrates expensive capacity where it adds value.

baseline.stem: Graph lookup and document retrieval do not depend on each other's output. How can wall-clock latency be reduced?

choices.text: Run them concurrently with bounded async calls

baseline.explanation: Independent I/O operations can execute concurrently.

baseline.stem: An optimization lowers average latency but doubles p95 and reduces faithfulness. Should it ship?

choices.text: No; evaluate tail latency, quality, and cost against explicit thresholds
Yes; average latency is the only metric

baseline.explanation: Production optimization is a multi-objective decision, not an average-only win.

reassessment.stem: A sprint goal says “connect everything” but reviewers disagree whether it is done. What was missing?
reassessment.explanation: Observable acceptance criteria create a shared definition of done.

reassessment.stem: Each subsystem has a separate CLI, but no request can pass through all of them. What should be implemented?

choices.text: An orchestrator composing graph, KT, RAG, and MCP

reassessment.explanation: An orchestrator establishes the unified runtime path.

reassessment.stem: Mocked module tests are green, yet mastery is not persisted after real tutor sessions. Which verification exposes this?

choices.text: An end-to-end simulation asserting state after the full flow
A unit test of an unrelated parser

reassessment.explanation: The defect crosses module and persistence boundaries.

reassessment.stem: The graph service fails, but vector retrieval remains healthy. Which behavior is preferable?

choices.text: Use the documented vector-only fallback and surface degraded status

reassessment.explanation: Transparent graceful degradation maintains safe partial service.

reassessment.stem: Clients must handle failures uniformly across three backends. Which boundary design helps?

choices.text: Translate backend exceptions into a stable tutor-domain error model
Expose vendor-specific stack traces as the API

reassessment.explanation: Domain-level errors decouple client behavior from backend details.

reassessment.stem: A request trace shows 5 seconds total but no stage timing. How should the next release improve it?

choices.text: Create correlated spans around retrieval, model, and tool calls

reassessment.explanation: Correlated child spans localize latency and failures.

reassessment.stem: Analysts need to compare low- and high-mastery sessions. Which trace field enables this?
reassessment.explanation: Mastery metadata supports cohort filtering.

reassessment.stem: A grader produces a 0.82 faithfulness result for a trace. Where does that value belong?

choices.text: A named score associated with the trace

reassessment.explanation: Named scores attach evaluation outcomes to observed executions.

reassessment.stem: A session replay reveals repeated unauthorized tool attempts. What makes the finding operationally useful?

choices.text: Annotate the trace and link the anomalous spans to follow-up

reassessment.explanation: Evidence-linked annotations support triage and remediation.

reassessment.stem: Median latency improves while users still report periodic 12-second waits. Which dashboard statistic is most likely to reveal them?
reassessment.explanation: A high percentile exposes slow-tail experiences hidden by central tendency.

reassessment.stem: Two model configurations have different prices and token usage. What unit makes the comparison actionable?

choices.text: Average cost for the same representative session workload

reassessment.explanation: A fixed session workload normalizes the cost comparison.

reassessment.stem: A curriculum document is unchanged across 1,000 queries. What avoids paying to embed it repeatedly?
reassessment.explanation: Stable content can safely reuse its existing embedding.

reassessment.stem: A cheap model matches the large model on intent classification but not hint quality. Which routing decision follows?

choices.text: Cheap model for intent; large model for hint generation
Cheap model for all hints without evaluation
Large model for trivial routing only

reassessment.explanation: Use evaluation evidence to assign models by task complexity.

reassessment.stem: Retrieval and learner-profile lookup each take 600 ms and are independent. What is the likely latency benefit of concurrent execution?

choices.text: Wall time approaches the slower call rather than their sum

reassessment.explanation: Concurrent independent I/O overlaps their waiting periods.

reassessment.stem: A cheaper routing policy saves 30% but causes golden-conversation regressions. What is the correct conclusion?

choices.text: Do not ship until quality meets the defined threshold
Ship because cost always dominates quality

reassessment.explanation: Cost savings are acceptable only within explicit quality constraints.

baseline.stem: You must choose a KT paper to reproduce in limited time. Which selection rationale is strongest?

choices.text: Key equations, public data, baseline metrics, and feasible compute are identifiable

baseline.explanation: A feasible reproduction needs recoverable methodology, data, metrics, and resources.

baseline.stem: A KT dataset is downloaded, but train/test splitting and sequence preprocessing are undocumented. What risk does this create?

choices.text: Results cannot be faithfully compared or reproduced

baseline.explanation: Data processing choices materially affect KT metrics and must be documented.

baseline.stem: A DKT model reports high accuracy on an imbalanced dataset. Which evaluation bundle better matches a rigorous protocol?

choices.text: AUC, accuracy, and RMSE computed with the paper's split protocol

baseline.explanation: Multiple metrics under a matched protocol support meaningful comparison.

baseline.stem: Your reproduced AUC differs from the paper. What should the comparison report do first?

choices.text: Document deviations in data, code, hyperparameters, and environment and assess their impact
Replace the result with the published number

baseline.explanation: Transparent deviation analysis is central to reproduction.

baseline.stem: A GraphRAG reproduction changes corpus, index settings, and query set simultaneously. Why is attribution weak?

choices.text: Multiple uncontrolled differences can explain any quality change

baseline.explanation: Controlled parity is needed to interpret reproduction outcomes.

baseline.stem: Which query allocation tests both entity-centered and corpus-wide GraphRAG behavior?

choices.text: A documented set containing local and global queries
Random prompts with no saved outputs

baseline.explanation: Local and global modes answer different classes of questions and both need evidence.

baseline.stem: Before submitting an OSS feature PR, which evidence most reduces maintainer review risk?

choices.text: Focused implementation, new behavior tests, full suite, lint, and clear test plan
A claim that tests are unnecessary

baseline.explanation: A scoped, tested change with a clear rationale is easier to review and trust.

baseline.stem: A two-hour workshop starts with slide creation before defining outcomes. What should backward design do instead?

choices.text: Define measurable objectives, align evidence, then choose activities and materials

baseline.explanation: Backward design begins with outcomes and aligned evidence.

baseline.stem: Half the workshop cannot finish setup. Which preparation would have most directly reduced this failure?

choices.text: Test setup on a fresh machine and pre-create environments or forks

baseline.explanation: Fresh-environment testing exposes setup assumptions before delivery.

baseline.stem: A portfolio says “built an AI tutor” but offers no evidence. Which update most strongly demonstrates expertise?

choices.text: A project page with architecture, demo, releases, research, OSS, and workshop artifacts
Hidden links with no alt text

baseline.explanation: Linked public artifacts convert claims into inspectable evidence.

reassessment.stem: Two KT papers are candidates: one has public code but unavailable data; the other has public benchmark data, equations, metrics, and manageable compute. Which is the safer reproduction target?

choices.text: The second, because the full experimental path is accessible
The first solely because code exists

reassessment.explanation: Accessible data and protocol are essential to an end-to-end reproduction.

reassessment.stem: A preprocessing script silently removes short learner sequences. What must accompany the reproduction results?

choices.text: The filtering rule, resulting dataset statistics, and impact on protocol parity
A claim that preprocessing never matters

reassessment.explanation: Transparent transformations and corpus statistics are needed to interpret results.

reassessment.stem: A neural KT run beats BKT on accuracy but loses on AUC. What is the defensible report?

choices.text: Report all prespecified metrics and avoid declaring a universal winner

reassessment.explanation: Prespecified multi-metric reporting prevents selective interpretation.

reassessment.stem: The paper used five seeds, but your experiment used one and obtained a higher score. What should you do?

choices.text: Run the matched seed protocol or clearly label the deviation and uncertainty

reassessment.explanation: Seed variance affects fair comparison and reproducibility.

reassessment.stem: Your GraphRAG index has different chunking from the paper default. Which next step best preserves interpretability?

choices.text: Record the deviation and run a parity configuration or controlled ablation

reassessment.explanation: A controlled comparison isolates the effect of changed chunking.

reassessment.stem: A system performs well on entity questions but poorly on whole-corpus synthesis. Which evaluation gap would a local-only test miss?

choices.text: Global search quality over community reports

reassessment.explanation: Corpus-wide synthesis specifically exercises global search.

reassessment.stem: CI fails only on the maintainer's supported Python version. What is the correct PR response?

choices.text: Reproduce the supported environment, fix the branch, add coverage, and document the change

reassessment.explanation: Contributions must satisfy the project's supported matrix and preserve the fix.

reassessment.stem: A workshop objective says “participants will know RAG,” but the lab asks them to build a cited tutor response. Which revision improves alignment?

choices.text: State that participants will build and validate a cited RAG response

reassessment.explanation: A measurable performance objective should match the planned evidence.

reassessment.stem: A mid-session pulse check shows most participants are stuck before the core lab. What should the facilitator do?

choices.text: Adapt pacing, address the common blocker, and preserve time for supported practice

reassessment.explanation: Formative feedback should drive real-time facilitation adjustments.

reassessment.stem: A portfolio architecture image has no alt text, the demo link is broken, and research claims lack artifacts. What is the highest-value remediation?

choices.text: Restore evidence links and add accessible descriptions, then rerun the site audit
Hide the project page from search

reassessment.explanation: A credible portfolio must make evidence both functional and accessible.
